dom: All-father, god of wisdom, war and the dead
sym: Spear Gungnir, two ravens, eight-legged horse
fa: Sacrificed one of his eyes at Mimir's well in exchange for wisdom

dom: God of thunder and protector of mankind
fa: His hammer Mjolnir always returned to his hand after being thrown

dom: Trickster god associated with mischief and chaos
fa: Could shapeshift into animals and was eventually bound in punishment for causing Baldur's death

dom: Goddess of marriage, motherhood and foresight
fa: Odin's wife, said to know everyone's fate but never reveal it

dom: Goddess of love, beauty, war and death
fa: Received half of all warriors who died in battle, with Odin receiving the other half

dom: God of fertility, prosperity and good harvests
fa: Owned a ship that could fold up small enough to fit in a pocket

dom: God of light, beauty and purity
fa: His death, engineered by Loki, was said to help trigger the events of Ragnarok

dom: God of law, justice and heroic glory
fa: Sacrificed his hand to bind the monstrous wolf Fenrir

dom: Watchman of the gods
fa: Guards the rainbow bridge Bifrost and will sound his horn to signal the start of Ragnarok

dom: God of the sea, wind and wealth
fa: Father of Freyr and Freyja, associated with safe sea travel and fishing

dom: Goddess who guards the apples of youth
fa: Her apples were said to keep the gods eternally youthful

dom: God of poetry and eloquence
sym: Harp, runes carved on his tongue
fa: Said to have runes carved onto his tongue at birth, granting him poetic skill

dom: Ruler of the underworld realm of the same name
fa: Depicted as half beautiful woman and half decaying corpse, ruling over those who die of sickness or age

dom: God of vengeance and silence
fa: Fated to avenge Odin's death at Ragnarok by killing the wolf Fenrir

dom: Goddess of winter, mountains and skiing
fa: Chose her husband among the gods by looking only at their feet

dom: Goddess associated with the earth and harvest
fa: Her golden hair, said to represent wheat fields, was cut off by Loki as a prank

dom: God of hunting, skiing and archery
fa: Was said to be so swift on skis that no one could catch him

dom: God or giant ruling over the sea
fa: Known for hosting legendary feasts for the gods deep beneath the waves

dom: Goddess of the sea who collects the drowned
fa: Said to use a net to drag sailors down to her underwater hall

dom: God of justice and reconciliation
fa: Presided over a hall where disputes among gods and men were peacefully settled

dom: God born specifically to avenge Baldur's death
fa: Grew to full size in a single day in order to avenge his brother Baldur

dom: Legendary hero and dragon-slayer
fa: Slew the dragon Fafnir and gained the ability to understand the speech of birds after tasting its blood

dom: Legendary Viking king and hero
fa: A legendary figure whose sagas mix historical raids with myth and legend

dom: Legendary master smith
fa: Crafted a pair of wings to escape captivity after being enslaved by a cruel king

dom: Female beings who shape the fate of gods and men
fa: The three Norns were said to weave the threads of fate at the base of the world tree Yggdrasil

dom: Female figures who choose who dies and lives in battle
fa: Carried chosen slain warriors to Odin's hall of Valhalla

dom: The immense world tree connecting the nine realms
fa: Its roots and branches were believed to connect all nine worlds of Norse cosmology

dom: Odin's majestic hall for slain warriors
sym: Golden hall, shields as roof tiles
fa: Said to have a roof made of interlocking shields, housing warriors preparing for Ragnarok

dom: The realm of the Aesir gods
fa: Connected to the human realm of Midgard by the rainbow bridge Bifrost

dom: The realm of humans in Norse cosmology
fa: Believed to be encircled by the vast sea serpent Jormungandr

dom: The prophesied end of the world and the gods
fa: A foretold final battle in which most of the major gods, including Odin and Thor, were fated to die

dom: The burning rainbow bridge linking Asgard and Midgard
fa: Guarded constantly by the god Heimdall against intruders

dom: The principal tribe of gods, including Odin and Thor
fa: Once warred with the Vanir gods before eventually making peace and merging pantheons

dom: A tribe of gods associated with fertility and prosperity
fa: Includes Freyr and Freyja, who joined the Aesir in Asgard after the Aesir-Vanir war

dom: Being renowned for wisdom, keeper of a sacred well
fa: Odin consulted his severed, preserved head for counsel even after Mimir's death

dom: Master craftsmen dwelling underground
fa: Said to have crafted many of the gods' greatest treasures, including Thor's hammer Mjolnir

dom: Supernatural beings associated with light or darkness
fa: Divided into light elves living in the sky and dark elves living underground

dom: A race of giants often in conflict with the gods
fa: Many gods, including Odin, had giant ancestry despite the frequent conflict between the two groups

dom: The primordial giant from whom the world was made
fa: His body was said to have been used by the gods to create the earth, sky and sea

dom: The first god, ancestor of the Aesir
fa: Said to have been licked out of a block of salty ice by a primordial cow named Audhumla

dom: The primordial cow who nourished the first beings
fa: Fed the giant Ymir with her milk while licking the first god, Buri, free from ice

dom: Personification of night
fa: Rides across the sky each night in a chariot pulled by a horse named Hrimfaxi

dom: Personification of day
fa: Rides across the sky each day in a chariot pulled by a shining horse named Skinfaxi

dom: Goddess who guides the sun's chariot
fa: Constantly pursued across the sky by a wolf who is fated to catch her at Ragnarok

dom: God who guides the moon's chariot
fa: Brother of Sol, also pursued eternally by a wolf across the heavens

dom: Being born from the gods' collective wisdom
fa: His blood was brewed into the legendary Mead of Poetry, said to grant the gift of poetry to whoever drinks it

dom: Giantess, mother of several of Loki's monstrous children
fa: Mother of Fenrir, Jormungandr and Hel with Loki

dom: Giantess who became Freyr's wife
fa: Freyr fell so deeply in love with her that he traded away his magic sword to win her hand

dom: Goddess associated with Baldur, his devoted wife
fa: Died of grief and was placed on the same funeral pyre as her husband Baldur

dom: Blind god who unknowingly killed Baldur
fa: Was tricked by Loki into throwing a mistletoe dart that killed his brother Baldur

dom: Dwarven brothers renowned as master smiths
fa: Crafted Thor's hammer Mjolnir in a wager against Loki, despite his repeated sabotage attempts

dom: Legendary dwarven craftsman
fa: Sometimes named as one of the dwarven smiths behind the gods' greatest treasures

dom: Legendary shieldmaiden and former Valkyrie
fa: Was placed in an enchanted sleep surrounded by fire until the hero Sigurd rescued her

dom: Legendary warrior granted an unnaturally long life
fa: Cursed to commit three great evil deeds in exchange for three lifetimes of a normal man

dom: Legendary hero and saga protagonist
fa: His saga tells of his love for the Valkyrie Sigrun and his eventual death and reincarnation

dom: Dwarven brothers who killed the wise being Kvasir
fa: Drained Kvasir's blood and mixed it with honey to create the legendary Mead of Poetry

dom: Fire giant destined to battle the gods at Ragnarok
fa: Prophesied to set the entire world ablaze with his sword at the end of Ragnarok

dom: Wise giant who competed with Odin in a contest of knowledge
fa: Engaged Odin in a deadly quiz contest, wagering his own head on the outcome

dom: Giant who guarded the Mead of Poetry
fa: Hid the Mead of Poetry inside a mountain, guarded by his daughter Gunnlod

dom: Giantess who guarded the Mead of Poetry
fa: Was tricked by Odin, who seduced her to steal the Mead of Poetry

dom: Mortal man aided by the goddess Freyja
fa: Was magically disguised as a boar by Freyja so he could safely learn about his ancestry

dom: Giantess and seeress consulted about lineage and fate
fa: Recited long genealogies of legendary heroes when questioned by Freyja

dom: Mysterious figure whose burning triggered the Aesir-Vanir war
fa: Her repeated burning and rebirth is said to have sparked the first war between the two tribes of gods

dom: Freyr's messenger and servant
fa: Was sent to woo the giantess Gerd on Freyr's behalf, using threats and magic to win her over

dom: Ancient earth goddess linked to fertility
fa: Worshipped with a sacred wagon procession, later thought to be an earlier form of the god Njord

dom: Goddess who witnesses oaths and agreements
fa: Believed to punish anyone who broke a sworn oath or promise

dom: Goddess of wisdom and careful observation
fa: Said to be so wise and attentive that nothing could be hidden from her

dom: Goddess who guards doors and defends against false claims
fa: Invoked in legal disputes to help deny false accusations

dom: Goddess associated with permission to marry
fa: Said to help lovers overcome obstacles and gain permission to wed

dom: Goddess who inspires love and affection
fa: Believed to turn people's thoughts and hearts toward love

dom: Goddess associated with plowing and the land
fa: Said to have plowed a piece of Sweden so deeply it broke away to form the island of Zealand

dom: Goddess associated with healing and medicine
fa: Regarded as the best of healers among the goddesses

dom: Goddess of wisdom, self-control and good manners
fa: Represented gentleness and prudent behavior among the Norse goddesses
